summary: Implemented the first-time onboarding flow with welcome steps (4 intro screens explaining plan/workout/record/progress), template selection (3 template cards: PPL, Upper/Lower, Full Body with descriptions), and plan configuration (review exercises before creation). The onboarding page orchestrates the multi-step flow: welcome -> template select -> plan config -> create plan and navigate to calendar. Skip button available at every step. Settings page already has 新手引导 link to navigate back.
keyDecisions: Multi-step flow uses useState for top-level step (0=welcome, 1=template_select, 2=plan_config) with welcome sub-steps (0-3) managed separately
TEMPLATES constant exported from onboarding service for direct component access (already static data)
OnboardingScreen accepts props interface for dependency injection (onCreatePlanFromTemplate, onMarkOnboardingComplete, onSkipOnboarding)
PlanConfig component shows review-only view of template exercises with sets notation (e.g. 4x5) - no weight editing since templates have null weights
Used existing UI patterns: Card-like containers, TouchableOpacity buttons, consistent Colors/Typography/Spacing from design system

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Welcome page: 3-4 intro steps explaining core concepts (plan/workout/record/progress)
Template selection: 3 template cards (PPL, Upper/Lower, Full Body) with descriptions
Plan configuration: adjust weights for template exercises before creation
完成 creates plan from template and navigates to calendar
跳过 skips onboarding entirely, navigates to calendar
Onboarding only shows on first use (checks OnboardingService.isOnboardingComplete)
Settings page 新手引导 link resets onboarding and navigates here
Template exercises pre-filled with default increments and rest times

notes: Settings page already had the 新手引导 link (NavigationRow) that navigates to /onboarding. The OnboardingService.isOnboardingComplete() check and resetOnboarding() were already implemented in the service layer. The page uses props-based dependency injection matching the pattern used by other screens (CalendarScreen, SettingsScreen).
